title: machaeranthera coulteri var. arida
intro: Machaeranthera coulteri var. arida, also known as Coulteri's tansy aster, is a rare and shrubby member of the sunflower family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, as well as promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Machaeranthera coulteri var. arida can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
